defs: The joint next above the hock, and near the flank, in the hind leg of the horse and allied animals; the joint corresponding to the knee in man; -- called also stifle joint. See Illust. under Horse.

records.ety: [Freq. of OE. stif stiff; cf. Icel. stīfla to dam up.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Stifled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Stifling (?).]

defs: To stop the breath of by crowding something into the windpipe, or introducing an irrespirable substance into the lungs; to choke; to suffocate; to cause the death of by such means; as, to stifle one with smoke or dust.
quotes: Stifled with kisses, a sweet death he dies.
I took my leave, being half stifled with the closeness of the room.

defs: To stop; to extinguish; to deaden; to quench; as, to stifle the breath; to stifle a fire or flame.
quotes: Bodies . . . stifle in themselves the rays which they do not reflect or transmit.

defs: To suppress the manifestation or report of; to smother; to conceal from public knowledge; as, to stifle a story; to stifle passion.
quotes: I desire only to have things fairly represented as they really are; no evidence smothered or stifled.

defs: To die by reason of obstruction of the breath, or because some noxious substance prevents respiration.
quotes: You shall stifle in your own report.
